{"key_event":"Indira centralization amid mass protest politics","reasoning":"The state gained coercive, fiscal, and developmental reach through war successes, bank nationalization, Green Revolution procurement, nuclear capability, and prime-ministerial centralization, but economic crisis and mounting unrest kept it from rising much beyond the already very high inherited level. Civil society strengthened through opposition victories after 1967, regional/caste mobilization, labor and student movements, judicial resistance, and the JP/Nav Nirman agitations, even as some autonomy was squeezed by economic controls and coercive suppression.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":8.1,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.3}
